input.description:
## Files
- `/Users/wballard/github/swissarmyhammer/swissarmyhammer-kanban/swissarmyhammer-kanban/src/board/init.rs:44-46`
- `/Users/wballard/github/swissarmyhammer/swissarmyhammer-kanban/swissarmyhammer-kanban/src/processor.rs:49`
- `/Users/wballard/github/swissarmyhammer/swissarmyhammer-kanban/swissarmyhammer-kanban/src/types/board.rs:39-57`

## What
The default column set (todo/doing/done) is defined in THREE separate places:
1. `Board::default_columns()` in `types/board.rs` -- returns `Vec<Column>` (the canonical one)
2. `default_columns()` in `board/init.rs:44` -- returns `Vec<(&str, &str, usize)>` tuples
3. An inline array literal in `processor.rs:49`

## Why
If the defaults ever need to change (add a "review" column, rename "To Do"), the change must be synchronized in three places. The tuple vs struct representations also diverge in type -- `init.rs` uses tuples converted to Entity, `processor.rs` uses inline tuples, while `board.rs` uses `Column` structs. This is a maintenance hazard.

## Suggestion
Keep only `Board::default_columns()` and convert from `Column` to `Entity` at the call site. Both `init.rs` and `processor.rs` should call `Board::default_columns()` and map to entities.

- [ ] Remove `fn default_columns()` from `board/init.rs`
- [ ] Remove inline column tuple from `processor.rs`
- [ ] Have both call `Board::default_columns()` and convert Column -> Entity
- [ ] Verify tests still pass

#warning"
input.title: Deduplicate default column definitions (3 copies)
